release: Australia release documentation on developer.servicenow.com; unversioned endpoint (latest)

form_fields.table_name: name of the table the file is attached to
form_fields.table_sys_id: sys_id of the record the file is attached to

file_part.name: uploadFile
file_part.filename: the attachment's file name, carried in the part's Content-Disposition
file_part.content_type: the file's own MIME type, set as the part's Content-Type header

metadata_list.url_encoding: the '^' conjunctions inside sysparm_query must arrive percent-encoded as %5E

metadata_fields.size_bytes: stringified byte count — the client decodes it to a number

error_envelope.client_rule: non-2xx responses raise SnowApiError with statusCode/message/detail from this envelope

rate_limit.delay_argument: Retry-After seconds * 1000, passed to the injected delay(ms) callable
rate_limit.direct_retry: 429 means the instance rejected the request before processing it, so the POST is retried directly with no correlation re-query
rate_limit.exhausted: after max_retries failed retries throw RateLimitError (a SnowApiError subclass) with .retryAfter in seconds

ambiguous_failure.statuses: 5xx or a transport error after the upload request was sent
ambiguous_failure.rule: never re-POST blindly: first re-query the correlation key; if an attachment with the expected size_bytes exists, return it without uploading again; only if nothing is found retry the POST

dedupe.rule: upload() queries the correlation key before posting; if the attachment already exists it is returned as-is and no second attachment is ever created

validation.rule: a 201 result must echo file_name/table_name/table_sys_id, and its size_bytes must equal the uploaded byte count; any mismatch throws AttachmentVerificationError
